patch.value: The package can still publish compiled JavaScript in dist because npm and Node execute JavaScript. Repository-authored implementation and test source should be TypeScript and compiled before execution.
Convert test source to typed TypeScript

Move the node:test coverage from a JavaScript .mjs source file to TypeScript, add a dedicated test compiler config, and run tests from compiled output so repository-authored code remains TypeScript-only.

- test/generator.test.ts replaces the JavaScript test source.
- tsconfig.test.json compiles tests with allowJs=false.
- npm test builds source, builds tests, and runs node --test on compiled test output.
- dist-test is ignored and never published.

value.author: codex
value.text: Converted test authoring plan opened after identifying test/generator.test.mjs as repository JavaScript source.

value.author: codex
value.text: Keep dist-test out of git and npm package contents.

value.author: codex
value.text: Node test execution can remain native by compiling TypeScript tests into an ignored dist-test tree.

value.author: codex
value.text: Initial test script path was corrected from dist-test/test to dist-test/*.js after Node treated the directory as a file; final release gate passed.

patch.value: Converted repository test authoring from JavaScript to TypeScript with compiled node:test execution.

test/generator.test.ts replaces test/generator.test.mjs, tsconfig.test.json compiles tests with allowJs=false into ignored dist-test output, npm test runs node --test dist-test/*.js, and npm run release:check passed with 18 tests.
Repository-authored test source is TypeScript, compiles with allowJs disabled, and the Node test suite remains green.
TypeScript test conversion implemented and verified
